blocked_state_policy: Maintain blocked state until new governance-facing evidence changes one or more threshold results. Do not reopen retest or paper trading on the basis of repeated refreshes that do not change threshold outcomes.

evidence_map.current_block_reason: The latest governance-facing overlap refresh still concludes overall_overlap_risk remains high and that redundancy signals remain active, especially around item5 and item8.
new_evidence_that_would_count: A newer governance-facing overlap artifact that no longer reports overall_overlap_risk = high.
An explicit statement that additional filter logic is no longer materially redundant versus the active baseline path.
A formal supersession statement saying the newer overlap artifact replaces the prior fail state for framework-governance purposes.
evidence_that_would_not_count: Re-reading the existing overlap audit without new findings.
General discussion about item6 or item7 being low overlap without changing the total overlap conclusion.
Any argument that implies overlap is acceptable without a new published governance-facing artifact.
evidence_map.minimum_trigger_to_reopen_assessment: A newly published overlap refresh artifact with changed conclusion or changed risk classification.

evidence_map.current_block_reason: The latest governance-facing sparsity-flatness refresh still says baseline_needs_entry_exit_redesign = true, best_hold_period_improves_current_coverage = false, and eligible ticker coverage remains too thin.
new_evidence_that_would_count: A newer trade-design or equivalent governance-facing audit that no longer concludes redesign is required for the active governance path.
Explicit evidence that coverage and flatness concerns are materially improved enough for governance purposes.
A formal supersession statement saying the newer trade-design refresh replaces the prior fail state.
evidence_that_would_not_count: Repeating the same trade-design audit result with unchanged coverage conclusions.
Using threshold 1 publication alone as evidence that sparsity-flatness is resolved.
Any informal claim that coverage is better without a new governance-facing artifact.
evidence_map.minimum_trigger_to_reopen_assessment: A newly published governance-facing trade-design refresh with materially different coverage/redesign conclusion.

evidence_map.current_block_reason: The latest governance-facing validation refresh still says the candidate remains experimental, cannot reopen Phase B retry after validation, and remains limited to framework-redesign-only use.
new_evidence_that_would_count: A newer validation artifact that no longer leaves the candidate in keep_candidate_experimental status for governance purposes.
Explicit evidence that the candidate is usable beyond framework-redesign-only scope on the governance path being assessed.
A formal supersession statement saying the newer validation refresh replaces the prior fail state.
evidence_that_would_not_count: Reusing the existing validation result that still says keep_candidate_experimental.
Treating framework-redesign-only usability as if it already satisfies governance clearance.
Any informal statement that the candidate seems good enough without a new published governance-facing validation basis.
evidence_map.minimum_trigger_to_reopen_assessment: A newly published governance-facing validation refresh or replacement decision with changed usability conclusion.

evidence_map.current_block_reason: The formal governance decision remains false because the three prerequisite refresh bases were all negative and closeout posture still says strategy experiments cannot continue now.
new_evidence_that_would_count: Updated published refresh bases for thresholds 1, 2, and 3 that materially change the governance picture.
A newer formal governance decision artifact that explicitly sets official_strategy_retry_reopened = true or otherwise changes the blocked state.
An explicit statement in the newer decision that the governance reopening decision is being re-evaluated on changed threshold evidence.
evidence_that_would_not_count: Running the same formal decision again on unchanged negative threshold bases.
Treating publication of threshold refreshes alone as automatic reopening.
Any operational argument that skips the formal governance decision step.
evidence_map.minimum_trigger_to_reopen_assessment: At least one of thresholds 1-3 must have genuinely changed evidence basis, followed by a new formal governance decision artifact.

global_non_triggers: Repeating the same refresh cycle without changed evidence.
Refreshing readiness artifacts while framework_governance_gate is still not cleared.
Opening atr_stop_corporate_action_policy while this blocker is still maintained as blocked.
Opening retest_governance_artifacts_sync while this blocker is still maintained as blocked.
what_must_change_before_gate_can_move: At least one threshold source-of-truth must change on genuinely new governance-facing evidence.
If threshold 4 is revisited, it must be revisited on changed threshold evidence, not the same blocked basis.
Only after four threshold outcomes are re-evaluated on changed evidence can framework_governance_gate be formally reassessed.
